what_it_does: A build-readiness audit of the episodic-memory loop-prep documentation set, cross-checking it against the real clone-base code and harvest sources to identify gaps, corrections, and new open decisions before an autonomous build loop runs.
observations: The verdict is that the docs are largely buildable and well-grounded, but contain a handful of silent-wrong-result blockers.
It lists seven top blocking gaps (B-1 through B-7) that could make a build pass criteria while being broken.
It lists nine secondary gaps and corrections (S-1 through S-9) to specify before relevant phases.
It surfaces five new open decisions (ND-1 through ND-5) not present in OPEN-DECISIONS.md.
It includes a 'what the docs get right' section to preserve trust in the underlying build-map and harvest citations.
It uses an evidence convention of Observed vs Inferred and avoids Verified claims because recollection does not yet exist.
Every disposition is cross-checked to a file:symbol citation.
standouts: B-1: sidechain exchanges are already captured but hard-filtered out at search time in src/search.ts, so a capture-only fix would silently fail recall.
B-2: tool_result is never populated for Claude in the DB, so 'fetch on demand via key' must mean re-reading the source JSONL archive, not querying tool_calls.tool_result.
B-3: dist/ is committed and the MCP server runs from dist/mcp-server.js, so any src edit without npm run build makes verification run stale bundled code.
B-6: replacing the summarizer removes one recursion source, but adding a SessionStart floor injection reintroduces the same trigger and could re-spawn fan-out.
B-7: sqlite-vec virtual table dimension is baked into the CREATE statement, so demoting FLOAT[384] requires per-lens table recreation, not additive ALTER.
conventions: Evidence convention: Observed = read directly; Inferred = pattern-matched; no Verified claims.
Issues are grouped BLOCKING-first, then secondary, then new decisions.
Each item has Observed facts, a 'why blocking' or 'why misleading' rationale, and a concrete Action.
The audit distinguishes capture layer from search/retrieval layer.
concerns: The file relies heavily on external code symbols that are not reproduced inside it.
Some 'Lean' recommendations are presented as decisions without showing the decision authority or approval.
The boundary between 'blocking' and 'secondary' is asserted rather than derived from a rubric in the file.
It does not define how the autonomous loop will ingest or enforce these findings.
notes: A code-grounded pre-loop audit that flags seven silent-failure blockers in the episodic-memory loop-prep docs before an autonomous builder starts.
questions: What exact threshold distinguishes a 'silent wrong result' from an explicit failure?
How will the autonomous build loop consume and enforce this audit?
Has the 'Lean' for each new decision been ratified, or is it still a proposal?
What is the precise conversation/session/file count N referenced in B-4?
What is the scope of the autonomous build loop's verification harness?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to answer whether an autonomous build loop would be blocked or misled by the loop-prep docs; the file does exactly that via a verdict, blocking gaps, secondary corrections, and new decisions.
apparent_themes: Separation of capture and retrieval layers
Schema migration rigor for virtual tables
intention_signals: It tries to act as an interpretive safety layer that prevents an autonomous builder from treating well-written but partially inaccurate docs as ground truth, by injecting real-code cross-checks and concrete fixes.
novelty: It treats loop-prep documentation itself as a code/design substrate and audits it against the actual clone-base before loop execution, rather than treating docs as authoritative.
contribution: A neutral, file-local readiness checkpoint that a fused system could keep to surface code-grounded blockers, secondary specifications, and unresolved build-time decisions without ranking or canonizing any single solution.
summary_for_embedding: A build-readiness audit of episodic-memory loop-prep documentation listing seven blocking gaps, nine secondary corrections, and five new open decisions against the real clone-base code and harvest sources.
intention_for_embedding: To catch silent-wrong-result mismatches between loop-prep docs and actual code before an autonomous build loop begins.
